customer_summary.summary: The frontend of the application is being upgraded to Angular 19. As part of that upgrade the SASS compiler that Angular uses has been deprecated and must be switched to the new Angular‑Builder implementation (@angular-devkit/build-angular:application). This migration is required now because the old compiler will no longer be supported in Angular 19, which would cause builds to fail and could introduce regressions in the UI styling. If the migration is not performed, the next scheduled Angular upgrade will break the CI build pipeline and users may see broken or unstyled pages.

developer_context.big_picture: Our product is a web‑based platform built with Angular that serves internal and external users who interact with the UI to perform business processes. The UI lives in the *presentation* container of the overall system (5 containers, ~1000 components). The upcoming Angular 19 release brings many performance and security improvements, but it also removes the legacy SASS compiler. This ticket ensures that the UI build pipeline continues to work after the Angular upgrade by migrating to the new SASS compiler provided by the Angular Builder. The migration must happen now because the Angular 19 upgrade is scheduled for the next release cycle; postponing it would block the upgrade and risk broken builds and visual regressions.
developer_context.scope_boundary: IN: All Angular frontend projects, build configuration files (angular.json, webpack/custom builders), SASS/SCSS source files, CI/CD build scripts that invoke the Angular builder, and UI test suites (Karma, Playwright). OUT: Backend Java services, domain logic, data‑access layer, infrastructure services unrelated to the UI build, and any non‑Angular front‑ends.

context_boundaries.boundary: Angular 19 drops the legacy SASS compiler; the project must switch to @angular-devkit/build-angular:application or builds will fail.
All @angular/* packages are currently at v18‑lts. Upgrading them to v19 may introduce incompatibilities with third‑party libraries that rely on older Angular APIs.
The UI test suites (Karma, Playwright) must be re‑run after the migration to catch any styling regressions caused by the new compiler.
CI pipelines that invoke the Angular build (currently using Webpack 5) may need adjustments if the new builder changes the build hooks or output paths.
Node.js version used on build agents must satisfy the minimum required by Angular 19 (typically Node 18+). Verify the build environment before migration.

developer_context.architecture_notes: The migration lives entirely in the **presentation** container (one of the five system containers). Within that container the relevant layer is **presentation** (≈287 components). The key component is the Angular application module, which is built by the Angular CLI using the build configuration defined in *angular.json*. This configuration references the SASS compiler via the builder. Neighboring components include: • UI component library (Angular Material, CDK) – unchanged; • Shared style assets (SCSS files) – will be re‑compiled; • CI/CD pipeline (infrastructure container) – triggers the Angular build; • Test runners (Karma, Playwright) – consume the compiled CSS. The developer’s work will modify the build configuration and possibly adjust import statements in SCSS files, but will not touch business‑logic components in the application or domain layers.

anticipated_questions.question: Do we need to upgrade the Angular CLI and all @angular/* packages to v19 before changing the SASS compiler?
anticipated_questions.answer: Yes. The new SASS compiler is only available with the Angular 19 build tools. The migration should be performed as part of the overall Angular 19 upgrade, which includes updating @angular/core, @angular/common, etc., and the Angular CLI.

anticipated_questions.question: Will existing SCSS files need to be rewritten?
anticipated_questions.answer: In most cases no, but the new compiler enforces stricter import rules (e.g., deprecation of the old `@import` syntax). Review the ADR for any required changes and run the UI tests to verify styling.

anticipated_questions.question: Is there any impact on the CI build agents?
anticipated_questions.answer: The agents must run a Node version compatible with Angular 19 (Node 18+). Also verify that the CI scripts still point to the correct builder target (`@angular-devkit/build-angular:application`).

anticipated_questions.question: Do we need to adjust our Karma or Playwright configurations?
anticipated_questions.answer: No direct changes are required, but after the migration you should re‑run the full UI test suite to catch any regressions introduced by the new compiler.

anticipated_questions.question: What is the fallback if the migration breaks the build?
anticipated_questions.answer: You can temporarily revert the Angular packages to v18 and the builder configuration to the previous setting, then investigate the specific incompatibility. However, the long‑term plan is to complete the Angular 19 upgrade.

linked_tasks: UVZ-09-ADR-003 (Frontend Build Strategy SASS Import Deprecation)
